title: RC Health
description: Regional Cluster health dashboard. Provides an at-a-glance view of the RC: fleet-wide HCP SLA, Hyperfleet component health, monitoring stack status, node health, Karpenter provisioning, and Kubernetes workload health.

panels.title: Management Clusters
panels.description: Count of Management Clusters actively reporting metrics to Thanos. Each MC hosts HCP control planes for the region.

panels.title: Total HCPs
panels.description: Total Hosted Control Planes across all Management Clusters in the region.

panels.title: HCPs Available
panels.description: Number of HCPs currently reporting as available (KubeAPIServerAvailable = True).

panels.title: HCPs Unavailable
panels.description: HCPs currently reporting as unavailable, excluding clusters that are installing or being deleted.

targets.expr: count(hcp:hostedcluster_available == 0 unless on(namespace) hcp:lifecycle_installing unless on(namespace) hcp:lifecycle_deleting) or vector(0)

panels.title: Median HCP Availability (30d)
panels.description: Median 30-day availability across all HCPs in the region. The SLA target is 99.95%.

panels.title: Median Error Budget Remaining
panels.description: Median error budget remaining across all HCPs. 100% means no availability loss; 0% means the 99.95% SLA budget is fully consumed over 30 days.

panels.title: HCPs Below SLA Target
panels.description: Number of HCPs whose 30-day availability is below the 99.95% SLA target.

panels.title: HCP Availability Distribution (30d)
panels.description: 30-day availability for each HCP in the region. Shows the spread across the fleet — lines clustering near 100% is healthy; outliers indicate problem clusters.

panels.title: Median Error Budget Burn Rate
panels.description: Median error budget remaining over time. A downward trend indicates fleet-wide availability degradation consuming the error budget.

panels.title: Pods Running
panels.description: Number of pods in Running phase across Hyperfleet platform namespaces.

panels.title: Pods Not Ready
panels.description: Number of pods in Hyperfleet namespaces that are not in a Ready state.

panels.title: Container Restarts
panels.description: Total container restarts across Hyperfleet namespaces in the last hour. Frequent restarts indicate crash-looping or OOM issues.

panels.title: Pod Restarts by Namespace
panels.description: Container restart rate per Hyperfleet namespace. Helps identify which service is restarting.

panels.title: Pods Not Ready by Namespace
panels.description: Count of not-ready pods per Hyperfleet namespace over time. Persistent non-zero values indicate degraded services.

panels.title: Prometheus Up
panels.description: Whether Prometheus instances are healthy. 1 = all up, 0 = at least one down.

panels.title: Thanos Components Up
panels.description: Fraction of Thanos components reporting as healthy.

panels.title: Alertmanager Up
panels.description: Whether Alertmanager instances are healthy. 1 = all up, 0 = at least one down.

panels.title: Prometheus Remote Write Errors
panels.description: Rate of failed samples being sent via remote write to Thanos. Should be zero — rising values indicate the metrics pipeline is dropping data.

panels.title: Prometheus TSDB Head Series
panels.description: Number of active time series in Prometheus TSDB head block. Growth over time indicates increasing metric cardinality.

panels.title: Thanos Query Latency (p99)
panels.description: 99th percentile query latency for Thanos Query. High values indicate Thanos is struggling to serve queries, which affects all dashboards and alerting.

panels.title: Nodes Ready
panels.description: Number of nodes in Ready condition.

panels.title: Nodes Not Ready
panels.description: Number of nodes not in Ready condition. Any value above 0 requires investigation.

panels.title: Max Node CPU Utilization
panels.description: CPU utilization of the busiest node. If this is high, at least one node is under pressure.

panels.title: Max Node Memory Utilization
panels.description: Memory utilization of the busiest node. If this is high, at least one node is under memory pressure.

panels.title: Disk Pressure
panels.description: Number of nodes with DiskPressure condition. Any node with disk pressure may evict pods.

panels.title: CPU Usage by Node
panels.description: CPU utilization per node over time. Identify which nodes are hot.

panels.title: Memory Usage by Node
panels.description: Memory utilization per node over time.

panels.title: Node Conditions
panels.description: Node pressure conditions over time (MemoryPressure, DiskPressure, PIDPressure). Any non-zero value indicates node stress.

panels.title: Nodes Created
panels.description: Number of nodes provisioned by Karpenter in the last hour. High values may indicate node churn.

panels.title: Nodes Terminated
panels.description: Number of nodes terminated by Karpenter in the last hour. High values alongside high creation counts indicate churn.

panels.title: NodePool CPU Utilization
panels.description: Current CPU usage as a percentage of the NodePool CPU limit. Approaching 100% means Karpenter cannot provision more capacity.

panels.title: NodePool Memory Utilization
panels.description: Current memory usage as a percentage of the NodePool memory limit. Approaching 100% means Karpenter cannot provision more capacity.

panels.title: Node Churn
panels.description: Rate of node creation vs termination by Karpenter. Persistent high rates of both indicate instability — nodes being created and destroyed repeatedly.

panels.title: Pending NodeClaims
panels.description: NodeClaims that are not yet fulfilled. Persistent pending claims indicate Karpenter cannot provision capacity — possible causes: instance type unavailable, capacity limits reached, or cloud provider errors.

panels.title: Disruption Actions
panels.description: Karpenter voluntary disruption decisions — consolidation, drift detection, and expiration. Shows how actively Karpenter is reshuffling nodes.

panels.title: API Server Error Rate
panels.description: Percentage of API server requests returning 5xx errors over the last 5 minutes.

panels.title: Pending Pods
panels.description: Number of pods in Pending phase across all namespaces. Persistent pending pods indicate scheduling or resource issues.

panels.title: Failed Jobs
panels.description: Number of Kubernetes jobs currently in a failed state.

panels.title: OOMKilled Containers
panels.description: Containers whose last termination was due to OOM across all namespaces.

panels.title: API Server Request Rate
panels.description: Total API server request rate. Baseline for understanding throughput and spotting traffic spikes.

panels.title: API Server Latency (p99)
panels.description: 99th percentile API server request latency. Sustained high latency indicates API server or etcd stress.

panels.title: Container Restarts by Namespace
panels.description: Container restart rate across all namespaces. Identifies which namespaces have the most instability.

panels.title: Pending Pods Over Time
panels.description: Number of pending pods over time. Rising trends indicate growing scheduling or resource pressure.
